Area 2 Item: 6. Management and Development of Curriculum and Teaching
Unnamed: 9: The curriculum of the school conforms to relevant laws and regulations, and it is able to cultivate students' various abilities and promote their all-round development.
The curriculum has horizontal coherence and vertical progression. It attends to students' learning differences and enriches their learning experience.
The curriculum and teaching management system can perform the functions of organisation, coordination and supervision, so as to raise the quality of curriculum and teaching.
The evaluation of the curriculum and teaching enables the school to know the performance and effectiveness of its implementation, and feedback is provided for improvement.
Students have positive attitudes and motivation towards learning. They are able to apply different learning methods and resources in their learning, thus achieving their learning objectives.
Students are able to acquire knowledge and skills. They possess an innovative spirit, a critical thinking mindset and the ability to put theory into practice.
Students' academic performance meets expectations, and is developing steadily.

Teachers possess professional knowledge and good communication skills. They are able to create a positive classroom atmosphere, respond to different learning needs of students, and establish a good rapport with them.
Teachers are able to adopt appropriate teaching strategies to give student-centred lessons, promote classroom interaction, and assist students in developing their learning abilities.
The school is able to use diverse ways to assess students' learning, enhance learning and improve teaching according to assessment results.
